NKJV.1: Praise the LORD! Sing to the LORD a new song, And His praise in the assembly of saints.
NKJV.2: Let Israel rejoice in their Maker; Let the children of Zion be joyful in their King.
NKJV.3: Let them praise His name with the dance; Let them sing praises to Him with the timbrel and harp.
NKJV.4: For the LORD takes pleasure in His people;  He will beautify the humble with salvation.
NKJV.5: Let the saints be joyful in glory; Let them sing aloud on their beds.
NKJV.6: Let the high praises of God be in their mouth, And a two-edged sword in their hand,
NKJV.7: To execute vengeance on the nations, And punishments on the peoples;
NKJV.8: To bind their kings with chains, And their nobles with fetters of iron;
NKJV.9: To execute on them the written judgment—  This honor have all His saints. Praise the LORD!

ESV.1: Praise the LORD! Sing to the LORD a new song, his praise in the assembly of the godly!
ESV.2: Let Israel be glad in his Maker; let the children of Zion rejoice in their King!
ESV.3: Let them praise his name with dancing, making melody to him with tambourine and lyre!
ESV.4: For the LORD takes pleasure in his people; he adorns the humble with salvation.
ESV.5: Let the godly exult in glory; let them sing for joy on their beds.
ESV.6: Let the high praises of God be in their throats and two-edged swords in their hands,
ESV.7: to execute vengeance on the nations and punishments on the peoples,
ESV.8: to bind their kings with chains and their nobles with fetters of iron,
ESV.9: to execute on them the judgment written! This is honor for all his godly ones. Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: Praise the LORD! Sing to the LORD a new song, [And] His praise in the congregation of the godly ones.
NASB.2: Let Israel be glad in his Maker; Let the sons of Zion rejoice in their King.
NASB.3: Let them praise His name with dancing; Let them sing praises to Him with timbrel and lyre.
NASB.4: For the LORD takes pleasure in His people; He will beautify the afflicted ones with salvation.
NASB.5: Let the godly ones exult in glory; Let them sing for joy on their beds.
NASB.6: [Let] the high praises of God [be] in their mouth, And a two-edged sword in their hand,
NASB.7: To execute vengeance on the nations And punishment on the peoples,
NASB.8: To bind their kings with chains And their nobles with fetters of iron,
NASB.9: To execute on them the judgment written; This is an honor for all His godly ones. Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
Praise the LORD!
Sing to the LORD a new song.
Sing his praises in the assembly of the faithful.
NLT.2:
O Israel, rejoice in your Maker.
O people of Jerusalem, exult in your King.
NLT.3:
Praise his name with dancing,
accompanied by tambourine and harp.
NLT.4:
For the LORD delights in his people;
he crowns the humble with victory.
NLT.5:
Let the faithful rejoice that he honors them.
Let them sing for joy as they lie on their beds.
NLT.6:
Let the praises of God be in their mouths,
and a sharp sword in their hands —
NLT.7:
to execute vengeance on the nations
and punishment on the peoples,
NLT.8:
to bind their kings with shackles
and their leaders with iron chains,
NLT.9:
to execute the judgment written against them.
This is the glorious privilege of his faithful ones.
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: Praise Yahweh! Sing to Yahweh a new song, his praise in the assembly of the saints.
WEB.2: Let Israel rejoice in him who made them. Let the children of Zion be joyful in their King.
WEB.3: Let them praise his name in the dance! Let them sing praises to him with tambourine and harp!
WEB.4: For Yahweh takes pleasure in his people. He crowns the humble with salvation.
WEB.5: Let the saints rejoice in honor. Let them sing for joy on their beds.
WEB.6: May the high praises of God be in their mouths, and a two-edged sword in their hand;
WEB.7: To execute vengeance on the nations, and punishments on the peoples;
WEB.8: To bind their kings with chains, and their nobles with fetters of iron;
WEB.9: to execute on them the written judgment. All his saints have this honor. Praise Yah!

CSB17.1:
Hallelujah!
Sing to the LORD a new song, ⓕ
his praise in the assembly of the faithful. ⓖ
CSB17.2:
Let Israel celebrate its Maker;
let the children of Zion rejoice in their King. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
Let them praise his name with dancing
and make music to him with tambourine and lyre. ⓘ
CSB17.4:
For the LORD takes pleasure in his people; ⓙ
he adorns the humble with salvation. ⓚ
CSB17.5:
Let the faithful celebrate in triumphal glory;
let them shout for joy on their beds. ⓛ
CSB17.6:
Let the exaltation of God be in their mouths [1]
and a double-edged sword in their hands, ⓜ
CSB17.7:
inflicting vengeance on the nations
and punishment on the peoples, ⓝ
CSB17.8:
binding their kings with chains
and their dignitaries with iron shackles, ⓐ
CSB17.9:
carrying out the judgment decreed against them.
This honor is for all his faithful people. ⓑ
Hallelujah!

AMP.1:
Praise the LORD!
Sing to the LORD a new song,
And praise Him in the congregation of His godly ones (believers).
AMP.2:
Let Israel rejoice in their Maker;
Let Zion's children rejoice in their King.
AMP.3:
Let them praise His name with dancing;
Let them sing praises to Him with the tambourine and lyre.
AMP.4:
For the LORD takes pleasure in His people;
He will beautify the humble with salvation.
AMP.5:
Let the godly ones exult in glory;
Let them sing for joy on their beds.
AMP.6:
Let the high praises of God be in their throats,
And a two-edged sword in their hands,
AMP.7:
To execute vengeance on the nations
And punishment on the peoples,
AMP.8:
To bind their kings with chains
And their nobles with fetters of iron,
AMP.9:
To execute on them the judgment written.
This is the honor for all His godly ones.
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.1:
Hallelujah!
Sing to God a brand-new song,
praise him in the company of all who love him.
MSG.2:
Let all Israel celebrate their Sovereign Creator,
Zion's children exult in their King.
MSG.3:
Let them praise his name in dance;
strike up the band and make great music!
MSG.4:
And why? Because God delights in his people,
festoons plain folk with salvation garlands!
MSG.5:
Let true lovers break out in praise,
sing out from wherever they're sitting,
MSG.6:
Shout the high praises of God,
brandish their swords in the wild sword-dance—
MSG.7:
A portent of vengeance on the God-defying nations,
a signal that punishment's coming,
MSG.8:
Their kings chained and hauled off to jail,
their leaders behind bars for good,
MSG.9:
The judgment on them carried out to the letter
—and all who love God in the seat of honor!
Hallelujah!

NIV.1:
Psalm 149
Praise the Lord.
Sing to the Lord a new song,
his praise in the assembly of the saints.
NIV.2:
Let Israel rejoice in their Maker;
let the people of Zion be glad in their King.
NIV.3:
Let them praise his name with dancing
and make music to him with tambourine and harp.
NIV.4:
For the Lord takes delight in his people;
he crowns the humble with salvation.
NIV.5:
Let the saints rejoice in this honor
and sing for joy on their beds.
NIV.6:
May the praise of God be in their mouths
and a double-edged sword in their hands,
NIV.7:
to inflict vengeance on the nations
and punishment on the peoples,
NIV.8:
to bind their kings with fetters,
their nobles with shackles of iron,
NIV.9:
to carry out the sentence written against them.
This is the glory of all his saints.
Praise the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
Praise the LORD!
Sing to the LORD a new song,
his praise in the assembly of the faithful.
NRSVCE.2:
Let Israel be glad in its Maker;
let the children of Zion rejoice in their King.
NRSVCE.3:
Let them praise his name with dancing,
making melody to him with tambourine and lyre.
NRSVCE.4:
For the LORD takes pleasure in his people;
he adorns the humble with victory.
NRSVCE.5:
Let the faithful exult in glory;
let them sing for joy on their couches.
NRSVCE.6:
Let the high praises of God be in their throats
and two-edged swords in their hands,
NRSVCE.7:
to execute vengeance on the nations
and punishment on the peoples,
NRSVCE.8:
to bind their kings with fetters
and their nobles with chains of iron,
NRSVCE.9:
to execute on them the judgment decreed.
This is glory for all his faithful ones.
Praise the LORD!

NET.1: Praise the LORD! Sing to the LORD a new song! Praise him in the assembly of the godly!
NET.2: Let Israel rejoice in their Creator! Let the people of Zion delight in their king!
NET.3: Let them praise his name with dancing! Let them sing praises to him to the accompaniment of the tambourine and harp!
NET.4: For the LORD takes delight in his people; he exalts the oppressed by delivering them.
NET.5: Let the godly rejoice because of their vindication! Let them shout for joy upon their beds!
NET.6: May they praise God while they hold a two-edged sword in their hand,
NET.7: in order to take revenge on the nations, and punish foreigners.
NET.8: They bind their kings in chains, and their nobles in iron shackles,
NET.9: and execute the judgment to which their enemies have been sentenced. All his loyal followers will be vindicated. Praise the LORD!
